assessment.title: Session 7 Quiz – Math and Number Methods

material.mattext:
Session 7 Quiz – Math and Number Methods

Test your understanding of the Math object, number methods, and numeric operations.

Topics Covered: - Math.random, Math.floor, Math.ceil, Math.round - Math.max, Math.min, Math.abs - Number methods (toFixed, parseInt, parseFloat) - isNaN and Number.isInteger - Random number generation - Rounding and precision

Instructions: - 16 questions from the Session 7 question bank - 20 minute time limit - 3 attempts allowed - 16 points total (1 point per question)

After syncing the question bank to Canvas:
Controlling how data is accessed and modified in a class
To validate and control access to properties
They are required in all classes
The property is intended to be private/internal

What will this code do? ```js class BankAccount { constructor(balance) { this.balance = balance; } withdraw(amount) { if (amount > this.balance) return false; this.balance -= amount; return true; } } const acc = new BankAccount(100); acc.withdraw(150); ```
Using a method: account.withdraw(500) with validation
What's wrong with this setter? ```js set price(newPrice) { this._price = newPrice; } ```
What does this getter do? ```js get fullName() { return this._firstName + " " + this._lastName; } ```
Combines two properties into one computed value

What will this return? ```js class Product { constructor(price) { this._price = price; } get price() { return this._price; } set price(val) { if (val < 0) return; this._price = val; } } const p = new Product(50); p.price = -10; console.log(p.price); ```
Keeping internal details private and exposing only necessary methods
Can you still access _property directly even with underscore convention?
Prevents bugs by controlling data access

What will this do? ```js class User { constructor(age) { this._age = age; } set age(val) { if (val < 0 || val > 120) { console.log("Invalid age"); return; } this._age = val; } } const u = new User(25); u.age = 150; ```
Log "Invalid age" and keep age at 25
True or false: Encapsulation makes debugging easier.
What's the purpose of validation in methods?
Ensure data meets requirements before accepting it
What is the correct pattern for a random integer from 1 to 6?
What does this produce? ```js const pages = Math.ceil(53 / 10); console.log(pages); ```
Which method always rounds DOWN regardless of the decimal part?
What does this return? ```js Math.abs(budget - spent) // where budget = 500, spent = 620 ```
